reviews.review_description:
This app has been instrumental in helping me learn to sail. The graphics are amazing and look stunning when you’re out on the water. The controls make you feel like you’re operating a real boat.  I’ve been going through the progression of lessons that teach the basics and inside and outs of sailing. You can choose to just lazily sail around different locations using different boats or participate in races either against bots or other people’s in multiplayer. And the globe feature is really cool allowing you to sail anywhere in the world. I even signed up to race a boat across the Mediterranean against other sailors which takes place in real time.

I’m brand new to sailing and feel like this is going to give me a great head start once I take actual lessons in the real world. But I can see how experienced sailors can also enjoy this.
I have been using this app for a few years now. It helps me be a better racer in real life. I also use it to relax on my virtual boat. Highly recommended.

I'm an avid sailor and racer and really enjoy this app.  They put a ton of work into getting the physics right and it shows.  Fun to have different boats from yachts to Laser to catamaran to Wasp ... each with their different subtleties.  The daily racing is great as well.  Keep up the good work MarineVerse team!

Great sailing experience. Amazing what the dev has done with this. 

Great fun in multiplayer

reviews.review_title: Excited to see future updates as well
reviews.review_description: Sailing is one activity I've always wanted to learn. Im looking forward to what this may offer towards learning such an amazing past time for some... sport for others 😉

This app has been phenomenal, providing all the lessons I need to get on the water and helping me to understand the fundamentals. They seem genuinely dedicated to user experience and I love that in the free sailing I can bring up YouTube watch some videos and just sail slowly around keeping an eye on the boat and getting a feel for how the conditions impact what you need to do. Would love an option to keep a little map on that shows the position of the boat so I could memorise it!

developer_response.body: Thank you for sailing with us and the suggestion! ❤️

reviews.review_title: A great alternative to the real thing
reviews.review_description: I wanted to get into dingy sailing but the only sailing club within 100kms closed down. I recently gave this a go for only $10AU. 2 Cups of coffees worth. Money very well spent. I'm relearning sailing with this app. It's been almost 50 years since my school sailing lessons. This picks up from where that let off. My favourite boat is the racing yacht. I turned up the Wind speed to 21knots today for the first time. If you can't get into a sailing club, I'd recommend this app instead, without hesitation.
Do you want to keep your sailing skills up then this is the program you want to have. It actually helps you keep yourself tuned in when you go back to sailing.
If you love water sports in general but also sailing there's heaps hear for you.Small fast boats to large keel boats they got the physics right.Developers open to feed back and ideas just buy it

reviews.review_title: One of the most unique simulation apps
reviews.review_description:
I always loved the water, although I can’t swim. I am recovering from surgery and limited right now to the time and what I can do physically. I love all sports. 
MarineVerse allows me to simulate sailing on the waters. A sport that I enjoy but can’t accomplish now due to physical limits. How great is that! MarineVerse does what it says it accomplishes, true sailing and in different locations and types sailing vessels. I also, recommend the Kayaking app from MarineVerse. In its early stages, it is very relaxing and fun. 
Keep up the great work on these very uniqueapps.

I really like MarineVerse.

The physics of sailing is great!
I´m a skipper and sometimes sail regattas, therefore for me it is perfect training when I am not at the sea.

I am an infrequent sailor and I always like to brush up on my knowledge before I go on a trip. This is so much better than just reading and taking tests. Getting a chance to actually practice as much as I want before the next adventure is amazing! I’m a visual learner too so it’s really so helpful. Awesome job!!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much, Adeika! That’s exactly what we’re aiming for — helping sailors like you build confidence through hands-on, visual learning. Have a great next adventure! ⛵

reviews.review_title: Has been brilliant so far: learnt heaps!
reviews.review_description: My partner and I are learning to sail to buy a boat and sail around the world (eventually). This game has helped massively (especially when combined with our RYA day skipper qualifications and volunteering on folks boats for the day). Love it, a really practical cool way to access and learn about sailing! 😍

developer_response.body: Thanks James! That’s an epic goal — love that MarineVerse is part of your journey. Keep learning, keep dreaming, and fair winds on your future adventure around the world! 🌍⛵

reviews.review_description:
I'm a 61 year old, disabled sailor who rather misses the days of lake sailing he did in the past. I find this game incredibly immersive, and sailing feels natural right down to using telltales to trim my sails. I honestly don't quite understand it, but the ambient sounds they included have me honestly picturing or rather, FEELING like I am sailing. I almost can feel the light air and mists, it's really something else! 

Also, due to my disability, I find sitting and standing for any length of time difficult, and this simulation allows me to very comfortably play it while laying prone in bed. I have tested and returned a dozen games this month due to their failing in this category. Honestly, I hope at some point in the future Meta adds another category or two to the ones they have right now which seem to be limited to "Sitting, Standing, Roomscale"

Related to this is things related to other disabilities. Some games force you to turn physically, which might be difficult or impossible for many of us. This game provides alternate ways of turning and moving that are very easy to use.

I haven't looked at whether it can be played one handed or not yet. I know that some in our Facebook group "Disabled Quest 2/3 User Community" either only have one hand or due to an injury can only use one hand, so that ability would be helpful if it's not there already. 

To that end, other categories that would be nice to see Meta add in addition to "Sitting, Standing, Roomscale" might be "Wheelchair, One Handed, Xbox Controller". Again, these wishes include ALL Meta games and apps, and this one might not include these needs so far, but it's head and shoulders above 80-90% of Quest apps I have tested thus far.

The other thing I wholeheartedly endorse is the fact MarineVerse is working on a beta that includes circumnavigation of the globe. I can't wait to see what they do with that!

I am a keen Hobie sailor and the catamaran model (which I had to pay to test) sails like a dingy that looks like a cat. Very disappointing. The rest kept me interested for about an hour. The user experience implementation makes it frustrating to use. Glad to see an attempt at VR Sailing.

developer_response.body:
Hi LennieLightning,

Thank you for taking the time to share your thoughts! As a keen Hobie sailor, your feedback is incredibly valuable to us. Our goal is to create the most authentic and enjoyable VR sailing experience possible, and we want every boat—especially the catamaran—to feel right for sailors like you.

I would love to hear more details on what you feel could be improved! If you’re up for it, please join our MarineVerse Discord and share your insights directly with our me and community. Your expertise could help me refine the experience and make it better for everyone.

Here’s the invite link: https://discord.gg/marineverse

Looking forward to your thoughts—hope to see you on the water!

Fair winds,
Greg

reviews.review_description: It's not only a sailing game, it's Sailing Club. Great community, wonderful people, tournaments and everyday racing.  Sure... not everything works perfectly, but developers are extremely responsive and doing their best to make it perfect.  Just like in real life... everything breaks on boats from time to time.

This simulation greatly exceeded my most optimistic expectations.  I strongly recommend it.  The detail is amazing.  It all just feels right.  I have tried all kinds of sport simulations.  I used to think that table tennis was the sport best suited to VR (because less movement is required than for other sports such as, say, tennis).  I now think that sailing is the best sport for VR because you don't move around that much within the boat (you tend to just shift from one side of the boat to the other).  The feel of the boat moving through the water, the look of the water itself, the feel of the wind on the sails - all these details are brilliantly done.  Racing against bots or other human beings is excellent and a lot of fun.  They rightly emphasize that being able to sail a VR boat is not the same as being able to sail a real life boat.  And of course that's true.  But I'm sure that sailing in VR has improved my sailing skills - maybe even a lot.  It's a great tool for learning.  After a race, back in the club house, you can even stand around a kind of table and watch the whole race again, like a god looking down from on high, and look at all of the boats (represented by tiny models moving on the surface), doing whatever they did.  It's a brilliant touch and a perspective that no-one would ever get in the real world.

I've had a lot of fun but there's still a lot of stuff I haven't even tried.  So far, I've limited myself to dinghies and yachts, but there are other boat types too.

I'd say that it's great for beginners and experts alike.  If you would like to learn to sail, this is a great place to start.  There are tutorials and lessons.  They even teach you the fundamentals and the terminology.  It's all very well done.    

And if you can already sail - better still.   It's a lot of fun.

Outside is 83 cm of snow and after shoveling driway I can go and sail. It is well done I have finally learned port and starboard sides. I enjoy daily races and an amazing  playback table showing all the opponents and where I screwed up.

This app is both a great sailing app and a sound example of what VR can do. 

It’s great to get set up like you would in a dinghy or boat and use basic technique and knowledge to get the most out of the boat. 

If I could sail under the Sydney Harbour Bridge that I see in the distance it’d be a definite 5-stars. Some real world nav and different real-world locations would be worth the developers time. 

Great job.
Great way to learn and practice and have the opportunity to sail and race with some very skilled sailors. I love it!

The best sailing app  I I’ve ever seen. I wish they had this app back when I was sailing this loop I know how to put it around the markers start the races for real and also won a silver cup for first place at the Detroit bowl club regatta back in the 50 and now at 88 I am racing again. Only problem I have is now I get up to the boat  and cat’t get on to sal my i hope someone can help me  👍👍

developer_response.body: hey bigTduke - thank you for your review! If you have any questions or suggestions - join us on Discord chat server: https://discord.gg/marineverse

reviews.review_description: I found out about MarineVerse in the NauticEd training I am taking.  My assessment  - Wow!  I learned more about sailing from a few hours in your VR world than I have gleaned from several week long cruises in the San Juans with a friend.  Steering and trimming sails without fear of trashing my friend's boat was a kick.  I did take out the dock once just for fun though!

NauticEdIinternational Sailing Education has adopted MarineVerse globally for recommendation for all its students to become masters at sailing and docking. We also encourage all of our on the water instructors to promote virtual reality training prior to their students getting onto the water. We have found this vastly improves our students abilities and competence.

developer_response.body:
Ahoy Grant!

Thank you for your support! We are grateful for our partnership with NauticEd and excited about how we are revolutionizing sailing education together. It's fantastic to see the positive impact of VR training on student competence and skills.

Separately, racing in the app has been a fun way to engage with you and the community. We look forward to more thrilling races!

Best regards,
The MarineVerse Team

reviews.review_description: The sailing physics are great, but the whole concept is totally ruined by the money grabbing add ons and passes. Almost cost more than a RL boat.

developer_response.body:
Hi Steve,

Thank you for appreciating the physics - a lot of work went into it. We're always pushing to make it better.

For your initial $20, you're getting access to Dinghy & Yacht, Sailing Lessons, Time-Trials and free sailing including the newly released Akalana Islands. That's hours of fun and the content is yours for life. As a reference, that's about the same as a single sailing crew session here in Australia (insurance cost).

Extra content like our Catamaran DLC is standard in many games. We've priced it at $9.99, which we think is good value.

Now about the Sailing Pass. It's an optional extra that gets you Pro Racing, all the boats and advanced training content for $99.99 a year (before any discounts). To put this into perspective, a sailing club membership in Australia can cost between $300-$700 annually, and that's not including storage and maintenance.

The Sailing Pass also allows us to maintain and improve the app for years to come. You can learn more about the Sailing Pass here: https://www.marineverse.com/sailing-pass

VR Sailing is a niche, enthusiast activity. Every person who signs up for the Sailing Pass makes a huge difference, enabling us to do what we do.

We're committed to making our sailing experience as accessible as possible and we value the feedback from our community. If you have suggestions or concerns about pricing, feel free to chat with us and other users on our Discord: https://discord.gg/marineverse

Thank you for your support.

Greg

reviews.review_description:
My dad has a sailing license and he says this game is very realistic the sails are on point and the controls are also very similar.
Don’t listen to the other reviews the water has been updated and is more realistic.

developer_response.body: Thank you for sharing your dad's feedback on MarineVerse Cup! We're delighted to hear that he, as a licensed sailor, finds the game realistic and appreciates the accuracy of the sails and controls. We constantly strive to improve the game's features, including the water visuals, and it's great to know that our efforts are being recognized. We appreciate your support and hope you and your dad continue to enjoy the sailing experience!

reviews.review_description:
Don't go into this expecting a sailing simulation. I have real world sailing experience and I was hoping this game could fill the gap during the winter months, but unless you're just going to use this to learn basic concepts and kinda sorta put them into practice, you're not going to have a good time.

My biggest gripe are the controls. I should be able to move the tiller myself to steer the boat, however it just doesn't work at all. Even the devs know this since the game constantly recommends steering with the joystick than moving the tiller, killing any and all immersion the game might have had. Other issues:

Lame graphics.
Moving around boats is slow and tedious. 
No capsizing on dinghies. 
Confusing menu and interface. 
Dumb AI will constantly crash into you.
Crawling around in the floor to move from one side of the boat to the other is hard in the knees. 

I really can't recommend this unless you can somehow pick it up for $5 or get it for free or something just to tide you over until something better comes along. It just feels like one of those cheap mobile games that exist to quiz you on terms and teach you the basics of sailing.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for feedback MoykeBaud, appreciate detailed breakdown of things you would like to see improved. Feel free to also add/upvote suggestions here: https://feedback.marineverse.com/marineverse-cup

Our roadmap includes improving AI, and adding capsizing on higher skills levels. 

As for controls, Quest currently doesn't offer 360 tracking we would need to really make the tiller work. Hopefully feature headsets will be able to support that. In game, we have Yacht, that has the controls in front of you, so you can use the mixture of grabbing things and using thumb-sticks/laser pointer ( in real-life, on a yacht, you would probably have crew to do some of those jobs ).

reviews.review_title: Fun but not worth the full price
reviews.review_description: I have been playing most days for about 2 weeks now so i think I have a good idea of the game. first the good, it is fun, easy to get into, has some teaching ability and it is cool to race others. Unfortunately, it also has many issues. Clunky interface, bad graphics and animation even for quest 2 (Check out the seagull if you want a laugh), flat calm water all the time, boring location backdrops, races that get old quickly as they are all much the same, wind always from the same general direction, a system that rewards those players who wait until late into the session to play because you can actually watch exactly how the previous players have raced before you have finished their turns allowing them to basically cheat by copying and maybe improving on their times. As for the live multiplayer, the one time I saw enough people for a race and joined the race was full of bugs, some had a completely different race presented to them so it basically did not work. Next issue is the price of pro access, 14 EUR a month for access to 1 other boat and a few "PRO" races, I simply do not see the value anywhere, I am not looking to learn about sailboat racing, I already know it, I have been sailing for many years in my own 13m sailboat and raced lasers when I was young. SO in conclusion, I paid 20EUR for a mediocre game that really needs some polish. 5 EUR  is about its worth IMO. I really hope the developers continue to improve this game, it has great potential, it's just been released at the wrong price and before it is really ready.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback SpinDreams. Next update will be addressing some of the bugs you describe in multiplayer.

Feel free to add your feedback and suggestions here: https://feedback.marineverse.com/marineverse-cup

Hey SpinDreams - did you had a chance to check out the app recently? We have added a lot: https://www.marineverse.com/marineverse-cup/history

reviews.review_description:
As someone who has instructed sailing when I was younger I’ve missed sailing now for a number of years. I was excited to try this, the thing that takes away from it most though is the wind direction, when I sail I feel the wind, it’s natural, the only indicators of wind I could find in this game are the wind vane on the top of the mast and the change in speed when I make adjustments to the sail. This doesn’t feel intuitive and having to look up constantly takes away from my wnjoyment of the game, I wish there was an indicator lower on the boat or near eye level that would show this, even if the water reacted to the wind direction, anything to stop staring at the top of the mast. I don’t like the tiler in the dinghy, the extended arm doesn’t always interact the way it would in real life and it’s annoying, using controller buttons to steer feels like cheating and also takes away from the experience. I don’t like the subscription strategy after paying for the game, the subscription for other features is extremely expensive, the basic features are minimal. The signup process was long winded and involved taking off the headset a couple times which was very annoying.

The game has potential, I remember being back racing aiming for the best possible start, I remember flying around buoys and picking up speed, I hope they make improvements.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sailing with us. There is nothing quite like the feeling of wind when sailing in real life ⛵

We hope to improve how the wind can been experienced in the game, including on the water hints. For now, a couple of ideas how to see and hear the wind in the app as of today:
- If you focus on your boat mailsail, in particular, if you look at tell-tales, you can see if your sails are trimmed too tight or too loose. ( you can also look for flapping sails, and use it as a guide to trim the sails )
- Each boat has a compass, you can look for the "red" cone that shows you the "No-go zone" ( and hence, wind direction )
- You can look at other boats and their sails, to assess what wind direction they are experiencing
- In the race, bow of the committee boat shows the wind direction ( it can also be used to predict wind changes, as it spins a bit before the change hits your boat )
- There is a subtle directional sound for the wind, so if you look around, you may be able to hear where exactly is the wind blowing from. 

Lastly, this will not help with assessing the wind direction, but might improve immersion:  try setting up a simple "office fan" to blow the wind in front of you. It feels nice in VR, even if it's not necessarily matches the "in game" wind.

Happy sailing!
Greg

reviews.review_description: It’s a nice little game, but for the $19 I paid I expected a little more. Was really not pleased with having to go outside of VR and try to sign up for some membership, which I still have not been able to make work.  I own two sailboats, a J-24 and a Mason 44, and have sailed for many years on top of spending 30 years in the Navy. I’ve rarely been sea-sick ever…until I tried this game, but that may just be me. Anyway, it’s good for learning points of sail and other basics as well as simple racing but not nearly as immersive as I would expect for the price. If it was more like $5 then ok.

developer_response.body:
Hey Chris19871,

Please reach out to support@marineverse.com if you need help with account setup.

Additionally, if you have any more details on what you would like to see in a VR sailing app, feel free to let us know via e-mail or here: https://feedback.marineverse.com/marineverse-cup

reviews.review_description: Marineverse Cup is a glimpse of things to come.  Learning and training in virtual reality.  This take someone with no knowledge and experience and gets them not only up to speed on sailing terminology, but the basics and intermediate aspects of sailing itself.

developer_response.body:
❤️⛵

Thank you for sailing with us! Yes, there is still so much potential for VR sailing - this is just 1.0 - many more improvements to come.

reviews.review_title: Just a beginner here, but WOW!
reviews.review_description: Great tutorial to get you going, and there seems to be a logical progression to more difficult situations including racing. Like sailing in real life, it's easy to learn but difficult to master. The user community has frequent events/races that I look forward to participating in.

I absolutely love the concept. While still very rough, it has a lot of potential! It still feels to me very much like an adaptation of a non-6dof, non motion contoller game, but I'm sure it will realize its potential.

Some feedback (valid at the time of writing, will revise as the game develops):
-Dinghy needs a wake!
-Accelerations are too quick
-Love how the dinghy tilts when you move around. Would love to see a bit of that in the axial direction too. And maybe try to capture the difference in how it feels to sail in displacement or planing. The wake, the sound of the water changes.
-the tiller snaps back to center when released, which is unnerving when trying to play with the tiller. It should start going back to center gradually.


That's it for now. I'm absolutely looking forward to see how this evolves, I was missing some sailing in the Quest dearly. Thank you!
The game is s step in the right direction compared to VR Regatta. I will be gland if there will be more sailing arrays and more boats to sail.

This is by far the best sailing VR I've experienced, and they continue improving it all the time. It's a lot of fun being able to race people as well and it really helps make me a better sailor in real life. Definitely worth it!!

Actually it's not only a game, it gives me the experience of real sailing including the staring at the water, the waves, in the distance different kind of weather, clouds, islands and the feeling of being out there free.... You can teach yourself or others to sail because of the real technical details and behaviour of the elements and the boat, and the multiplayer option. As an experienced sailor you can improve your skills and race the daily races. This is fun and amazing for everyone, the real maximum immersive vr sailing.

I have been sailing for 50 years.  Racing in the Atlantic, Pacific, and Gulf Coast as well as inland.  It's a fun group and can be challenging especially those tricky wind shifts.  If you are a sailor or were in the past give this one a try.  Only thing missing is the smell and refreshing spray from the bow.

reviews.review_title: Great fun. Very hard at the beginning.
reviews.review_description: A great way to learn how to sail. The experience seems true to life even if the graphics are less than stellar. Tiny clouds at 100 feet? All in all, great fun!

I knew nothing about sailing and this is a good game to learn the basics.  There are apparently leagues and cups, but I’m still too much of a beginner to come close to reaching those levels.  It’s fun to sail though, and it definitely feels like practice makes better in this game.  I’ve watched a few sailing races on tv and always wondered about the hows and whys, and now I know!  SUPER annoying emails about the game all the time though, I’ve had to block them as there doesn’t seem to be option for no emails that I could find.

developer_response.body:
Hi Miktay811, welcome aboard! Glad that you are finding the app helpful at demystifying sailing. Can't wait to see you on the leaderboards!

You can control MarineVerse Cup e-mail preferences under "MarineVerse Cup" tab in your profile: https://www.marineverse.com/my-profile

reviews.review_description: It is a great game.

developer_response.body:
Thank you! It means a lot to hear it ❤️

This is the first review after the game graduated left App Lab 🎉

reviews.review_description: I've sailed but have done limited racing.  Currently this is one of my favorite VR games. Might just get me to buy a boat again!

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sailing with us ❤️ 

Hopefully MarineVerse Cup will inspire you to invest in a boat ⛵
